figures.caption: Figure 4: Qualitative comparison results on GoPro [1]. The proposed method produces realistic details and sharper results compared to restoration-based and diffusion-based methods.

Figure 1: Main concept of our DeblurFlow. Unlike conditional generative flow models that generate vector fields for deblurring, our method produces task-aligned residuals using a r-space encoder–decoder tailored for restoration, notably reducing GMACs.

Table 1: Quantitative comparison on GoPro. Metrics are grouped into restoration fidelity and perceptual realism. All methods are trained on the same dataset. We utilize 1-step sampling and NAFNet as the fidelity expert. The best results are indicated in bold.
Figure 9: Visual comparison results on RealBlur-J [32].

Figure 2: Overview of the proposed DeblurFlow framework. Our DeblurFlow produces a deblurringoriented vector field, i.e., residual error, which is preferred for deblurring tasks. The r-space encoderdecoder, connected by the residual shortcuts, enables fidelity-preserving residual learning while reducing computational cost from 422.7 to 19.5 GMACs compared to the VAE encoder–decoder.
Figure 7: Visual comparison results on real-world blur images.

Table 2: Performance comparison on HIDE [31] and RealBlur-J [32]. All methods are trained on the same dataset. We utilize 1-step sampling and NAFNet [7] as the fidelity expert. The best results are indicated in bold.
Figure 8: Visual comparison results on RWBI [33].
Figure 6: Ablation study on # of sampling steps. (a) restoration fidelity (PSNR, LPIPS) and (b) perceptual realism (NIQE, MUSIQ).
